# *arg*: Clean and Simple Argument Checking

## Overview

*arg* produces nice, clean error messages for checking function
arguments in R packages. I developed *arg* because I often found myself
writing the same error messages in my packages, and I wasn’t satisfied
with the other similar R packages available. *arg* uses
[*cli*](https://cli.r-lib.org/) to produce clean and clear errors
without requiring much programming from the developer. These messages
are designed to be clear to the user, not using complicated language.
Many messages adapt to the type of (incorrect) input received.

*arg* contains function for common arguments checks, such as checking
that an argument is a single number
([`arg_number()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_numeric.md)),
a string
([`arg_string()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_character.md)),
a single `TRUE`/`FALSE` value
([`arg_flag()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_logical.md)),
or a data frame or matrix
([`arg_data()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_data.md)).
In addition, it is possible to build more complex argument checks using
[`arg_and()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_or.md) and
[`arg_or()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_or.md), which
require that all or at least one check is passed, and
[`when_not_null()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/when_supplied.md)
and
[`when_supplied()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/when_supplied.md),
which allow for `NULL` or omitted arguments.

``` r
library(arg)

z <- "a string"

# Check that `z` is a string
arg_string(z)

# Check that `z` is a number
arg_number(z)
#> Error:
#> ! `z` must be a number.

# Check that `z` is a string of length 2
arg_and(z,
        arg_string,
        arg_length(2L))
#> Error:
#> ! All of the following conditions must be met:
#> ✔ `z` must be a string
#> ✖ `z` must have length 2

# Check that `z` is NA, a flag, or a count
arg_or(z,
       arg_is_NA,
       arg_flag,
       arg_count)
#> Error:
#> ! `z` must be NA, a logical value (TRUE or FALSE), or a count (a
#>   non-negative whole number).
```

*arg* is meant to be used inside other R packages. Its error-throwing
function [`err()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/err.md) (a
wrapper for
[`rlang::abort()`](https://rlang.r-lib.org/reference/abort.html))
automatically includes the relevant (user-facing) function in its error
message.

The name “arg” is meant to be short for “argument”, but also perhaps
represents the sound you would make if you were to encounter an error
when running some code. Hopefully *arg* reduces the number of times
users say “arg!”.

## Installation

You can install the development version of *arg* from
[GitHub](https://github.com/ngreifer/arg) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("ngreifer/arg")
```

You can install the stable version on CRAN using

``` r
install.packages("arg")
```

## Related packages

*arg* serves a similar function to other existing packages. These
packages may be better for your purposes, and *arg* is not meant to
compete with them.

- [*assertions*](https://selkamand.github.io/assertions/)
- [*checkmate*](https://mllg.github.io/checkmate/)
- [*checkarg*](https://cran.r-project.org/package=checkarg)
- [*chk*](https://poissonconsulting.github.io/chk/)
- [*dreamerr*](https://cran.r-project.org/package=dreamerr)
- [*erify*](https://flujoo.github.io/erify/)
- [*favr*](https://lj-jenkins.github.io/favr/)

# Package index

## Argument checking

- [`arg_between()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_between.md)
  [`arg_gt()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_between.md)
  [`arg_gte()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_between.md)
  [`arg_lt()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_between.md)
  [`arg_lte()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_between.md)
  : Check Values in Range
- [`arg_character()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_character.md)
  [`arg_string()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_character.md)
  [`arg_factor()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_character.md)
  : Check Character Argument
- [`arg_atomic()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_data.md)
  [`arg_vector()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_data.md)
  [`arg_list()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_data.md)
  [`arg_data.frame()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_data.md)
  [`arg_matrix()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_data.md)
  [`arg_array()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_data.md)
  [`arg_data()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_data.md)
  [`arg_env()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_data.md) :
  Check Common Argument Types
- [`arg_dots_supplied()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_dots_supplied.md)
  [`arg_dots_not_supplied()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_dots_supplied.md)
  : Check Supplied Dots Argument
- [`arg_element()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_element.md)
  [`arg_not_element()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_element.md)
  : Check Element
- [`arg_equal()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_equal.md)
  [`arg_not_equal()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_equal.md)
  : Check Equal Arguments
- [`arg_formula()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_formula.md)
  : Check Formula Argument
- [`arg_function()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_function.md)
  : Check Function Argument
- [`arg_index()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_index.md)
  [`arg_indices()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_index.md)
  : Check Index Argument
- [`arg_is()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_is.md)
  [`arg_is_not()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_is.md) :
  Check Argument Class
- [`arg_length()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_length.md)
  : Check Argument Length
- [`arg_logical()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_logical.md)
  [`arg_flag()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_logical.md)
  : Check Logical Argument
- [`arg_named()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_named.md)
  [`arg_colnamed()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_named.md)
  : Check Named Argument
- [`arg_no_NA()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_no_NA.md)
  [`arg_is_NA()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_no_NA.md)
  [`arg_all_NA()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_no_NA.md)
  : Check NA in Argument
- [`arg_non_null()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_non_null.md)
  [`arg_null()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_non_null.md)
  : Check NULL Argument
- [`arg_numeric()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_numeric.md)
  [`arg_number()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_numeric.md)
  [`arg_whole_numeric()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_numeric.md)
  [`arg_whole_number()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_numeric.md)
  [`arg_counts()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_numeric.md)
  [`arg_count()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_numeric.md)
  : Check Numeric Argument
- [`arg_or()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_or.md)
  [`arg_and()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_or.md) :
  Check That Arguments Meet Some or All Criteria
- [`arg_supplied()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_supplied.md)
  : Check Supplied Argument
- [`arg_symmetric()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_symmetric.md)
  [`arg_cov()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_symmetric.md)
  [`arg_cor()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_symmetric.md)
  [`arg_distance()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_symmetric.md)
  : Check Symmetric Matrix Argument
- [`arg_unique()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/arg_unique.md)
  : Check Unique Argument
- [`when_supplied()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/when_supplied.md)
  [`when_not_null()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/when_supplied.md)
  : Check Arguments When Supplied

## Argument processing

- [`match_arg()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/match_arg.md)
  : Argument Verification

## Assertions

- [`err()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/err.md)
  [`wrn()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/err.md)
  [`msg()`](https://ngreifer.github.io/arg/reference/err.md) : Throw an
  Error, Warning, or Message
